nodes.title: Bloch--Kato and \nek's Selmer groups
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.1.1.}
  For a number field $E$ and a geometric $p$-adic $G_E$-representation
  $V$, the \emph{Bloch--Kato Selmer group} is $H^1_f(E,V)$, an
  $L$-subspace of $H^1(G_E, V)$ defined by local conditions.

  \nek's theory of Selmer complexes yields, for nice $p$-adic families
  of $G_E$-representations (in particular for the sheaf $\cV$ over
  $\cX$ with the local condition at $p$ coming from
  \cref{lem:OrdinaryReductionAtP}), the Selmer groups
  $\tH^i_f(E, V)$ for all $i \geq 0$.  When $V$ is ordinary and not
  exceptional, $\tH^1_f(E, V) = H^1_f(E, V)$.

nodes.title: Theorem A: $p$-adic Beilinson--Bloch--Kato in analytic rank $1$
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:TheoremB}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §1.1 (discussion following Theorem~A).}
  Part~1 is an immediate consequence of \cref{thm:TheoremB}, the
  Jacquet--Langlands correspondence, and the local epsilon-factor
  observation: when $\varepsilon(V) = -1$ and $\Pi$ is ordinary,
  locally distinguished and non-exceptional, the height of the Heegner
  class $P_\Pi$ equals $e_{p\infty}(V)^{-1}\,\cL_p'(V,0)\,Q(\cdot)$,
  which is non-zero exactly when $\cL_p'(V,0) \neq 0$, exhibiting an
  explicit class in $\tH^1_f(E,V) = H^1_f(E,V)$ of non-zero height,
  whence $\dim_L \tH^1_f(E,V) \geq 1$.  Part~2 follows by invoking
  Fouquet's Euler-system bounds \cite{fouquet}, which generalise
  Kolyvagin's bounds on Tate--Shafarevich groups of elliptic curves and
  pin $\dim_L \tH^1_f(E,V) = 1$ together with the stated divisibility.

nodes.title: Number fields --- Mathlib anchor
Totally real field and CM extension
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.2.}
  Fix a rational prime $p$.  Let $F$ be a totally real field and let
  $E/F$ be a CM quadratic extension.  Set
  $G_0 := \Res_{F/\bQ}\mathrm{GL}_2$ and
  $H := \Res_{E/\bQ}\mathbf{G}_m$.  Let $L$ be a finite extension of
  $\bQ_p$ splitting $F$.

nodes.title: Quaternion algebra
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.1.}
  Let $\bB$ be a quaternion algebra over $F_{\bA}$ with ramification
  set $\Sg \sqcup \{v \mid \infty\}$ satisfying
  $|\Sg| \equiv [F:\bQ] - 1 \pmod{2}$.  Then
  $\bG(\bA) := \bB^\times$ is an \emph{incoherent} algebraic group over
  $\bQ$.  For every $v \in \Sg$ we require $E_v / F_v$ to be nonsplit;
  this guarantees a unique $\bB^\times$-conjugacy class of embeddings
  $E_{\bA} \hookrightarrow \bB$, of which we fix one, yielding
  $\mathrm{e} \colon H \hookrightarrow G$.

nodes.title: Incoherent groups $G$, $H$, $Z$, $(G \times H)'$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.1.}
  The groups of interest, collectively denoted $G_*$, are:
  \[
    G, \quad H, \quad G \times H, \quad (G \times H)' := (G \times H)/Z,
    \quad H' := H/Z,
  \]
  where $Z := \Res_{F/\bQ}\mathbf{G}_m$ is embedded diagonally, $G$
  is the incoherent group of \cref{def:QuaternionAlgebra}, and
  $H = \Res_{E/\bQ}\mathbf{G}_m$.

nodes.title: Shimura varieties
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.1.}
  Associated with the groups in \cref{def:GroupGH} and suitable Shimura
  data are towers of compactified Shimura varieties
  \[
    X_{/F},\quad Y_{/E},\quad (X \times_F Y)_{/E},\quad Z_{/E},\quad Y'_{/E}.
  \]
  The varieties $X$, $Z$, $X \times_F Y$ are curves; $Y$ and $Y'$ have
  dimension $0$.  The embedding $\mathrm{e} \colon H \hookrightarrow G$
  induces a diagonal embedding $H' \hookrightarrow (G \times H)'$, hence
  a morphism of Shimura varieties $\mathrm{e}' \colon Y' \to Z$.

nodes.title: Cuspidal cohomological automorphic representation
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.2.}
  Let $W$ be an algebraic representation of $G_{*,\infty}$ over $L$ and
  let $\cW$ be the corresponding \'{e}tale local system on the tower
  $X_*$.  A \emph{(cuspidal, cohomological) automorphic representation
  of $G_*(\bA)$ over $L$ of weight $W$} is a representation
  $\Pi = \Pi^\infty \otimes W$ of $G_*(\bA)$ occurring in
  $H^\bullet(X_{*,\overline{E}}, \cW^\vee) \otimes W$.

nodes.title: Ordinary automorphic representation
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.2.}
  An automorphic representation $\pi_0$ of $G_0(\bA)$ over $L$ is
  \emph{ordinary} if for all $v \mid p$, the associated
  $G_{F_v}$-representation $V_{\pi_0,v}$ reduces nontrivially as
  \[
    0 \to V_{\pi_0,v}^+ \to V_{\pi_0,v} \to V_{\pi_0,v}^- \to 0,
  \]
  with $G_{F_v}$ acting on $V_{\pi_0,v}^+$ via $\chi_{\mathrm{cyc}}
  \cdot \alpha_v^\circ$ where $\alpha_v^\circ$ takes values in
  $p$-adic units.

nodes.title: Galois representation $V_\Pi$ and the sheaf $\cV$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.3.}
  For $\Pi = \pi \otimes \chi$ a cuspidal automorphic representation of
  $(G \times H)'(\bA)$ over $L$, the associated $G_E$-representation is
  $V = V_\Pi := V_{\pi|G_E} \otimes V_\chi$.

  Over a locally distinguished Hida family $\cX$
  (up to replacing $\cX$ by an open subset containing $\cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$),
  there exists a locally free sheaf $\cV$ of rank $2$ with a
  $G_{E,Sp}$-action such that $\cV_{|z} \cong V_{\Pi_z}$ for all
  $z \in \cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ via the Langlands correspondence.

nodes.title: Locally distinguished
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.4, Definition (loc dist).}
  For each place $v$ of $F$, define the local epsilon factor
  \[
    \varepsilon_v^G(V) := \varepsilon(V_v)\,\chi_v(-1)\,\eta_v(-1)\,
    \varepsilon(\bB_v) \;\in\; \{\pm 1\}.
  \]
  An automorphic representation $\Pi$ of $(G \times H)'(\bA)$ is
  \emph{locally distinguished by $H'$} if $\varepsilon_v^G(V) = +1$
  for all places $v$.

nodes.title: Global toric period pairing $Q$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.4.}
  For $\Pi$ locally distinguished, the local toric period
  $Q_{v,dt_v}(f_{1,v} \otimes f_{2,v} / f_{3,v} \otimes f_{4,v})$ is
  an explicit generator of $\Pi_v^{*,H'_v} \otimes (\Pi_v^\vee)^{*,H'_v}$
  normalised by the local $L$-factor $\mathscr{L}(V_{(\pi,\chi),v},0)$
  and the Haar measure $dt_v$.  Choosing measures with
  $\mathrm{vol}(H'(\bQ)\backslash H'(\bA), dt) = 1$, the \emph{global
  toric period pairing} is
  \[
    Q\!\left(\frac{f_1 \otimes f_2}{f_3 \otimes f_4}\right)
    := \prod_v Q_{v,dt_v}\!\left(\frac{f_{1,v} \otimes f_{2,v}}
    {f_{3,v} \otimes f_{4,v}}\right)
  \]
  for $f_1 \in \Pi_{H'}$, $f_2 \in \Pi^\vee_{H'}$, $f_3 \in \Pi$,
  $f_4 \in \Pi^\vee$ with $\prod_v (f_{3,v}, f_{4,v})_v \neq 0$.

nodes.title: $p$-adic height pairing
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.5.}
  Fix a Galois-equivariant pairing $V \otimes V^\iota \to L(1)$ where
  $V^\iota := V_{\Pi^\vee}$.  The ordinary filtrations at $w \mid p$
  from \cref{lem:OrdinaryReductionAtP} satisfy the exactness condition
  that $V_w^+$ and $V^{\iota,+}_w$ are exact orthogonal complements.
  This data determines a \emph{$p$-adic height pairing}
  \[
    h_V \colon \tH^1_f(E,V) \otimes \tH^1_f(E,V^\iota)
    \longrightarrow L \,\hat\otimes\, \GF
  \]
  on \nek's Selmer groups.

  Over the Hida family $\cX$, the interpolated duality
  $\cV \otimes \cV^\iota \to \scO_\cX(1)$ and the family of filtrations
  yield a height pairing
  \[
    h_{\cV/\cV^\iota} \colon
    \tH^1_f(E,\cV) \otimes_{\scO_\cX} \tH^1_f(E,\cV^\iota)
    \longrightarrow \cN^*_{\cX/\cX^0}
    \cong \scO_\cX \,\hat\otimes\, \GF,
  \]
  where $\cX^0$ denotes the base Hida family for $G_0 \times H$.

nodes.title: Heegner cycle $P_W$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.6.}
  Assume the weight condition~(wt).  The map $\mathrm{e}' \colon Y' \to Z$
  induces, for every $V' \subset H'(\bA^\infty) \cap K$, a canonical
  map $H_0(Y'_{V'}, L) \to H_0(Z_K, \cW)$.  The image
  $\Delta^\circ_{W,f_\infty} \in H_0(Z_K, \cW)_0$ of the normalised
  fundamental class $[Y'_{V'}] = |Y'(\overline{E})|^{-1}\sum_{y \in
  Y'(\overline{E})} [y]$ lies in the kernel of
  $H_0(Z_K, \cW) \to H_0(\overline{Z}_K, \cW)$.  The images under the
  Abel--Jacobi maps
  $\mathrm{AJ} \colon H_0(Z_K, \cW)_0 \to H^1(E, H_1(\overline{Z}_K,
  \cW))$ are tower-compatible and $H'(\bA)$-invariant, hence define
  \[
    P_W := \varprojlim \mathrm{AJ}(\Delta^\circ_{W,-})
    \;\in\; \varprojlim_K H^1\!\left(E,\,
    H_1(\overline{Z}_K, \cW)^{H'(\bA)}\right).
  \]

nodes.title: Heegner functional $P_\Pi$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.3.6.}
  Via the Carayol injection
  $\Pi \hookrightarrow \varinjlim_K \mathrm{Hom}_{L[G_E]}(H_1(\overline{Z}_K,
  \cW), V_\Pi)$, the class $P_W$ defines an $H'(\bA)$-invariant
  functional
  \[
    P_\Pi \colon \Pi_{H'_\infty} \longrightarrow H^1(G_E, V_\Pi).
  \]
  When $\Pi$ is ordinary and not exceptional, the image of $P_\Pi$
  lies in $H^1_f(E, V_\Pi)$.
  The composition $P_\Pi^{\ord} := P_W \circ \gamma^{\ord}_{H'}$ of
  $P_\Pi$ with Hida's ordinary projector operator $\gamma^{\ord}_{H'}$
  is the \emph{ordinary Heegner functional}.

nodes.title: Theorem B: $p$-adic Gross--Zagier formula for a fixed representation
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, Theorem~B (§1.3).}
  Let $\Pi = \pi \otimes \chi$ be an ordinary, locally distinguished,
  non-exceptional automorphic representation of $(G \times H)'(\bA)$
  over $L$.  Let $V = V_\Pi$.

  The image of $P_\Pi$ lies in $H^1_f(E, V)$, and for all
  $f_1 \in \Pi_{H'_\infty}$, $f_2 \in \Pi^\vee_{H'_\infty}$,
  $f_3 \in \Pi$, $f_4 \in \Pi^\vee$ with $(f_3, f_4)_\Pi \neq 0$:
  \[
    \frac{h_V(P_\Pi(f_1),\, P_{\Pi^\vee}(f_2))}{(f_3, f_4)_\Pi}
    =
    e_{p\infty}(V_{(\pi,\chi)})^{-1}
    \cdot \cL_p'(V_{(\pi,\chi)}, 0)
    \cdot Q\!\left(\frac{f_1 \otimes f_2}{f_3 \otimes f_4}\right),
  \]
  where $e_{p\infty}(V_{(\pi,\chi)}) \in L^\times$ is the
  $p$-interpolation factor for $\cL_p(V_{(\pi,\chi)},s)$.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:TheoremD, thm:TheoremC}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §7.1.}
  The proof is completed in §7.1 by specialising \cref{thm:TheoremD} to a classical point.
  Fix a classical point $z \in \cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ corresponding to $\Pi$; the right-hand
  side of the universal formula (\cref{thm:TheoremD}) specialises to
  $e_{p\infty}(V)^{-1} \cdot \cL_p'(V, 0) \cdot Q(\cdot)$ by the interpolation property
  of $\mathrm{d}^\sharp \cL_p$ at classical points.  The height pairing
  $h_{\cV/\cV^\iota}$ specialises to $h_V$ by §5.3; the universal Heegner class
  $\cP_{K^{p\prime}}$ specialises to $P^\ord_{\Pi_z}$ by \cref{thm:TheoremC}.  The
  image of $P_\Pi$ lies in $H^1_f(E,V)$ by Proposition 6.4.7 (valid when $B_p$ is split
  and $\Pi$ is ordinary and not exceptional).  For representations of trivial weight
  satisfying the technical conditions (p-crys) and (ram), the formula is
  \cite[Theorem~B]{nonsplit}; Proposition~7.1.6 (``final lemma'') shows this implies the
  general case via the density of such points.

nodes.title: Hida family for $(G \times H)'$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.4.2.}
  Fix an open compact $K^p \subset (G \times H)'(\bA^{p\infty})$.  Let
  $\cE^{\ord}_{K^p} := \mathrm{Spec}\,\mathbf{T}^{\mathrm{sph},\ord}_{K^p,\bQ_p}$
  be the ordinary eigenvariety for $(G \times H)'$, with dense classical
  locus $\cE^{\ord,\mathrm{cl}}$.  A \emph{Hida family} is an irreducible
  component $\cX \subset \cE^{\ord}_{K^p}$; set
  $\cX^{\mathrm{cl}} := \cX \cap \cE^{\ord,\mathrm{cl}}$.

  The Hida family $\cX$ is \emph{locally distinguished} if for every
  classical point $z \in \cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ (of weight satisfying the
  weight condition~(wt)), the attached Galois representation
  $\cV_z$ satisfies $\varepsilon_v^G(\cV_z) = +1$ for all
  $v \nmid p\infty$.

nodes.title: Ordinary filtration of $\cV$ at $p$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.4.3.}
  For each $w \mid p$ there is an exact sequence of
  $\scO_\cX[G_{E_w}]$-modules
  \[
    0 \to \cV_w^+ \to \cV_w \to \cV_w^- \to 0,
  \]
  where $\cV_w^\pm$ are line bundles over $\cX$, specialising to the
  ordinary filtration $0 \to V_w^+ \to V_w \to V_w^- \to 0$ at all
  $z \in \cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$.

nodes.title: Universal Heegner class $\cP_{K^p}$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.4.4.}
  The ordinary Heegner class $P^\ord_{W,K^{p\prime}} := P_{W,K^{p\prime}} \circ
  \gamma^\ord_{H'}$ is $H'(\bA^{p\infty})$-invariant.  Its localisation
  over $\cX$ (in the case $W$ trivial) is a section of
  $\tH^1_f(E, \cM_{K^{p\prime}}^{H'_\Sg})$, where the subscript $f$
  uses the local condition from \cref{lem:OrdinaryReductionAtP}.
  Via the Carayol map over $\cX$, this section defines an
  $\scO_\cX$-module map
  \[
    \cP_{K^{p\prime}} \colon
    \Pi^{K^{p\prime},\ord}_{H'_\Sg}
    \longrightarrow \tH^1_f(E, \cV),
  \]
  the \emph{universal Heegner class}.

nodes.title: Theorem C: Universal Heegner class interpolates Heegner cycles
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, Theorem~C (§1.4).}
  Let $\cX$ be a locally distinguished Hida family for $(G \times H)'$.
  There exist an open subset $\cX' \subset \cX$ containing
  $\cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ and a map of sheaves over $\cX'$
  \[
    \cP_{K^{p\prime}} \colon
    \Pi^{K^{p\prime},\ord}_{H'_\Sg}
    \longrightarrow \tH^1_f(E_{Sp}, \cV),
  \]
  satisfying:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item $\cP_{K^{p\prime}}$ is invariant under the away-from-$p\Sg$
          Hecke algebra of $H'$;
    \item for all $z \in \cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ with $\Pi_z$ satisfying~(wt),
          $\cP_{K^{p\prime}|z} = P^\ord_{\Pi_z, K^{p\prime}}$ under the
          natural map
          $\tH^1_f(E_{Sp},\cV)_{|z} \to H^1(G_{E,Sp}, V_{\Pi_z})$.
  \end{enumerate}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:HeegnerCycle, def:HeegnerFunctional, lem:OrdinaryReductionAtP}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §6.3--6.4 and Proposition~A.2.4.}
  The map $\cP_{K^{p\prime}} = P_{W,K^{p\prime}} \circ \gamma^\ord_{H'}$ is
  $H'(\bA^{p\infty})$-invariant because both $P_{W,K^{p\prime}}$ (by construction)
  and $\gamma^\ord_{H'}$ (a Hecke-equivariant projector) are; this gives part~(1).
  For part~(2), via Proposition~3.2.5, the class $\cP_{K^{p\prime}}$ can be viewed as an
  $\scO_\cX$-linear functional into $H^1(G_{E,Sp}, \cV)$ over the open $\cX^{(3,f)}$;
  the local Selmer conditions at $w \nmid p$ (Lemma~6.4.1) and at $w \mid p$ using the
  filtration from \cref{lem:OrdinaryReductionAtP} (Lemma~6.4.3) force $\cP$ into the
  Selmer group $\tH^1_f(E,\cV)$.  The interpolation property
  $\cP_{K^{p\prime}|z} = P^\ord_{\Pi_z, K^{p\prime}}$ follows from the definition of
  $\cP_W$ in §6.3 and the weight-independence result (Proposition~6.3.2), which
  identifies $\gamma^\ord_{H'}$ as a limit of $p^\infty$-Hecke operators intertwining
  toric and ordinary parts (Proposition~A.2.4).

nodes.title: $p$-adic $L$-function $\cL_p(\cV^\sharp)$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.5.2, Theorem (the main theorem of \cite{dd-pLf}).}
  Let $\cX^\sharp$ denote the Hida family for $G \times H$ overlying
  $\cX$, and let $\cX_0^\sharp := \iota_{\mathrm{JL}}(\cX^\sharp)
  \subset \cE^{\sharp,\ord}_{G_0 \times H}$.  There exists a meromorphic
  function
  \[
    \cL_p(\cV^\sharp) \in \cK(\cX_0^\sharp)
  \]
  whose polar locus does not meet $\cX_0^{\mathrm{cl}}$, uniquely
  characterised by the interpolation property: for each classical
  $z = (x,y) \in \cX_0^{\sharp,\mathrm{cl}}$ outside the polar locus,
  corresponding to $\pi_x \otimes \chi_y$ with weights $(\underline{w}_x,
  \underline{l}_y)$ satisfying $|l_{y,\sigma}| < w_{x,\sigma}$ and
  $|w_x + l_y| \leq w_{x,\sigma} - |l_{y,\sigma}| - 2$ for all
  $\sigma \colon F \hookrightarrow \mathbf{C}$, one has
  \[
    \cL_p(\cV^\sharp)(x,y)
    = e_{p\infty}(V_{(\pi_x, \chi_y)}) \cdot
    \mathscr{L}(V_{(\pi_x, \chi_y)}, 0).
  \]

nodes.title: Cyclotomic derivative $\mathrm{d}^\sharp \cL_p(\cV)$
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §1.5.3.}
  Under the local distinction hypothesis on $\cX$, the function
  $\cL_p(\cV^\sharp)$ vanishes identically on $\cX_0$.  The conormal
  sheaf
  $\cN^*_{\cX_0/\cX_0^\sharp} = \mathcal{I}_{\cX_0}/\mathcal{I}_{\cX_0}^2
  \otimes_{\scO_{\cX_0^\sharp}} \scO_{\cX_0} \cong \scO_{\cX_0}
  \,\hat\otimes\, \GF$
  carries the \emph{cyclotomic derivative}
  \[
    \mathrm{d}^\sharp\cL_p(\cV) :=
    \mathrm{d}_{\cX_0/\cX_0^\sharp}\,\cL_p(\cV^\sharp)
    \;\in\; \cK(\cX_0)\,\hat\otimes\,\GF
    \;=\; \cK(\cX)\,\hat\otimes\,\GF.
  \]

nodes.title: Theorem D: Universal $p$-adic Gross--Zagier formula
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, Theorem~D (§1.5).}
  Let $\cX$ be a locally distinguished Hida family for $(G \times H)'$.
  Abbreviate $\Pi^{(\iota)} := \Pi^{K^{p\prime},\ord,(\iota)}_{H'_\Sg}$,
  $\scO := \scO_\cX$, $\cK := \cK_\cX$.

  There exists an open subset $\cX' \subset \cX$ containing
  $\cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ over which all constructions can be made, and
  \[
    \frac{h_{\cV/\cV^\sharp}(\cP(f_1),\, \cP^\iota(f_2))}{((f_3, f_4))}
    =
    \mathrm{d}^\sharp \cL_p(\cV^\sharp) \cdot
    \cQ\!\left(\frac{f_1 \otimes f_2}{f_3 \otimes f_4}\right),
  \]
  as an equality of $\cK\,\hat\otimes_{\bZ_p}\,\GF$-valued
  $\scO$-linear functionals on
  $(\Pi \otimes_\scO \Pi^\iota) \otimes_{\scO^\times}
  (\Pi \otimes_\scO \Pi^\iota)^{\times,-1}$.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:TheoremB, thm:TheoremBord, def:CyclotomicDerivative}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §7.1.}
  The proof occupies §7.1.  Both sides of the formula are
  $\scO_{\cX'}$-linear functionals on
  $(\Pi \otimes_\scO \Pi^\iota) \otimes_{\scO^\times} (\Pi \otimes_\scO
  \Pi^\iota)^{\times,-1}$: the left side because $h_{\cV/\cV^\iota}$ is
  $\scO_\cX$-bilinear (from \cref{def:HeightPairing}) and $\cP(f_1)$ is
  $\scO_\cX$-linear in $f_1$ (from the Carayol map); the right side
  because $\mathrm{d}^\sharp\cL_p(\cV^\sharp)$ is a meromorphic function on $\cX$ and
  $\cQ$ is $\scO_\cX$-linear.  Since both sides agree at every classical
  point $z \in \cX^{\mathrm{cl,n\text{-}exc}}$ satisfying~(wt) by
  \cref{thm:TheoremB} (established for special test vectors via the
  ordinary-variant \cref{thm:TheoremBord}, then for all vectors by
  multiplicity-one), and $\cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ is Zariski dense in $\cX'$
  (Lemma~7.1.4, derived from the density of $\cX^{\mathrm{cl,p\text{-}crys,}\bQ_p,\mathrm{ram}}$
  in $\cX'$), the difference of the two sides vanishes identically.

nodes.title: Theorem E: Iwasawa main conjecture for derivatives
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, Theorem~E (§1.6.1).}
  Let $\cX$ be a locally distinguished Hida family for $(G \times H)'$
  satisfying the further conditions of Fouquet~\cite[Theorem~B(iii)]{fouquet}.
  Let $\cX' \subset \cX$ be the open subset of \cref{thm:TheoremD},
  assumed regular up to shrinking.  Let
  $\mathcal{R} \subset \scO_{\cX'}\,\hat\otimes\,\GF$ be the regulator
  of the height pairing over $\cX'$.  Then
  \[
    \mathrm{d}^\sharp\cL_p(\cV)
    \succeq_{\scO_{\cX'}}
    \mathcal{R} \cdot
    \mathrm{char}_{\scO_{\cX'}}
    \!\left(\tH^2_f(E,\cV)_{\scO_{\cX'}\text{-tors}}\right).
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:HeightPairing}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §1.6.1 and §7.1.}
  The proof is virtually identical to that of \cite[Theorem~D]{dd-pyzz},
  based on Theorem~C.4 \emph{ibid.} and \cite[Theorem~B(ii)]{fouquet}.
  From \cref{thm:TheoremD}, the cyclotomic derivative
  $\mathrm{d}^\sharp\cL_p(\cV)$ equals the height pairing of the universal Heegner
  class divided by the toric period $\cQ$.  The regulator
  $\mathcal{R} = \det(h_{\cV/\cV^\iota})$ measures the size of the
  $\scO_{\cX'}$-module generated by $\cP$ in
  $\tH^1_f(E,\cV)$.  Fouquet's theorem \cite[Theorem~B(iii)]{fouquet}
  provides an upper bound on the characteristic ideal of
  $\tH^2_f(E,\cV)_{\scO_{\cX'}\text{-tors}}$ in terms of the Selmer
  class; inserting the universal formula gives the stated divisibility.

nodes.title: Theorem F: Generic non-vanishing of $p$-adic heights for CM motives
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, Theorem~F (§1.6.2).}
  Let $\mathscr{Y} \subset \cE^{\ord,\mathrm{sd}}_H$ be an irreducible
  component of type $-1$ (i.e.\ all classical points $y \in \mathscr{Y}$
  have $\varepsilon(1, \chi_y) = -1$).  Suppose all primes $v \mid p$
  of $F$ split in $E$, the extension $E/F$ is ramified, and
  $p \nmid 2 D_F h_E^-$.

  Then there exists a nonempty open subset $\mathscr{Y}' \subset
  \mathscr{Y}$ such that for all $y \in \mathscr{Y}^{\mathrm{cl}}
  \cap \mathscr{Y}'$, the Selmer group $H^1_f(E, \chi_y)$ is nonzero
  and the $p$-adic height pairing
  \[
    h \colon H^1_f(E, \chi_y) \otimes H^1_f(E, \chi_y^{-1}(1))
    \longrightarrow \bQ_p(y)\,\hat\otimes\,\GF
  \]
  is non-vanishing.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:PAdicLFunction}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §7.1 (proof of Theorem~F) and §1.6.2.}
  Let $\mathscr{Y}^\sharp \subset \cE^{\ord}_H$ be the component containing $\mathscr{Y}$.
  For each $p$-adic CM type $\Sigma$ of $E$ there is a Katz $p$-adic $L$-function
  $L_\Sigma \in \scO(\mathscr{Y}^\sharp)$ interpolating the algebraic parts of
  $L(1, \chi_y)$.  Under the stated hypotheses, Burungale \cite{ashay} proves that the
  normal derivative $\mathrm{d}^\sharp L_\Sigma \in \mathscr{N}^*_{\mathscr{Y}/\mathscr{Y}^\sharp}$
  is non-vanishing; let $\mathscr{Y}' := \bigcap_\Sigma \mathscr{Y}'_\Sigma$ be the
  complement of its zero loci.  For any $y \in \mathscr{Y}^{\mathrm{cl}} \cap
  \mathscr{Y}'$, a factorisation argument via a theta-lift $\pi_0 = \theta(\chi\chi_0)$
  and the anticyclotomic main conjecture (Hida--Hsieh) provides a class
  $Z \in H^1_f(E, \chi_y) \otimes H^1_f(E, \chi_y^c)$ (using \cref{thm:TheoremD}
  applied to the corresponding Hida family) whose $p$-adic height is non-vanishing;
  the vanishing of $H^1_f(E, \chi')$ forces $Z$ to lie in the desired factor.

nodes.title: Theorem G: Non-vanishing of universal Heegner classes along classical Hida families
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, Theorem~G (§1.6.3).}
  Let $\cX_0$ be a Hida family for $\mathrm{PGL}_{2/\bQ}$ and let
  $\cX_0^\sharp$ be the Hida family for $\mathrm{GL}_{2/\bQ}$
  containing $\cX_0$.  Denote by $\cV_0$, $\cV_0^\sharp$ the associated
  rank-$2$ representations of $G_{\bQ}$.

  Suppose $\cX_0$ contains a point corresponding to an elliptic curve
  $A$ with split multiplicative reduction at $p$ and $L(A, 1) \neq 0$.
  Then:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item The universal Heegner class $\cP_0$ is nonvanishing along
          $\cX_0$.
    \item The Selmer group $\tH^1_f(\bQ, \cV_0)$ has generic rank $1$,
          generated by $\cP_0$.
    \item The $p$-adic height pairing $h_{\cV_0/\cV_0^\sharp}$ is
          non-vanishing.
  \end{enumerate}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:TheoremC}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §7.3 (end of §7.3).}
  Let $E$ be an imaginary quadratic field such that $p$ is inert in $E$, all other
  primes dividing the conductor of $A$ split in $E$, and $L(A_E, 1) \neq 0$.  Then $A$
  has split multiplicative reduction over $E$ with Tate parameter
  $q = q_A \in E_p^\times$.  By the main result of Bertolini--Darmon \cite{BDrigid}
  (as reformulated in \cite[Theorem~5.4]{bdsurvey}), there is $c \in \bQ_p^\times$ such
  that $\cP_{0,E}(x_0) \otimes \cP_{0,E}^\iota(x_0) = c \cdot (L(A_E,1)/\Omega_{A_E})
  \cdot [q] \otimes [q]$ in
  $\tH^1_f(\bQ, V_pA_E) \otimes \tH^1_f(\bQ, V_pA_E)$.  Since $L(A_E,1) \neq 0$ and
  $[q] \neq 0$ (Stickelberger--Tate), the universal Heegner class $\cP_0$ is
  non-vanishing at $x_0$; by \cite{nek-koly}, $\tH^1_f(\bQ, \cV_0)$ has generic rank
  $1$, generated by $\cP_0$.  The height
  $h_{\cV_0/\cV_0^\sharp}(\cP_0, \cP_0^\iota)(x_0) = c \cdot \ell(q) \cdot
  L(A_E,1)/\Omega_{A_E}$ is non-zero by \cite{st et} (Greenberg--Stevens), establishing
  part~(3); parts~(1) and~(2) follow from the non-vanishing of $\cP_0$ and the
  Nekov\'{a}\v{r}--Kolyvagin argument, and \cref{thm:TheoremC} ensures the
  specialisation from the family level to the classical level.

nodes.title: Theorem $\mathrm{B}^{\ord}$: $p$-adic Gross--Zagier formula for ordinary forms
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, Theorem~$\mathrm{B}^{\ord}$ (§7.1, ``GZ thm'\,'').}
  Let $\Pi = \pi \otimes \chi$ be an ordinary, locally distinguished
  automorphic representation of $(G \times H)'(\bA)$ over $L$.  Let
  $V = V_\Pi$ and let
  $\tilde P^\ord_\Pi \in \tH^1_f(E, V_\Pi)$ be the enhanced ordinary
  Heegner class.

  Then for all $f_1 \in \Pi^\ord_{H'_\infty}$,
  $f_2 \in \Pi^{\vee,\ord}_{H'_\infty}$, $f_3 \in \Pi^\ord$,
  $f_4 \in \Pi^{\vee,\ord}$ with $(f_3, f_4)^\ord \neq 0$:
  \[
    \frac{h_V(\tilde P^\ord_\Pi(f_1),\, \tilde P^\ord_{\Pi^\vee}(f_2))}
         {(f_3, f_4)^\ord_\Pi}
    =
    \cL_p'(V_{(\pi,\chi)}, 0)
    \cdot
    Q^\ord\!\left(\frac{f_1 \otimes f_2}{f_3 \otimes f_4}\right).
  \]
  In contrast to \cref{thm:TheoremB}, this variant also holds for
  \emph{exceptional} $\Pi$.  When $\Pi$ is not exceptional, it is
  equivalent to \cref{thm:TheoremB} (via the comparison of special
  quadruples).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:TheoremB}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §7.1 (Lemma B-Bord and final lemma).}
  When $\Pi$ is not exceptional, Lemma~7.1.2 (``B Bord'') shows the
  formula~\eqref{formula B'} for $(f_1,f_2,f_3,f_4)$ is equivalent to
  \cref{thm:TheoremB} for the associated `special quadruple'
  $(f_1', f_2', f_3', f_4')$ obtained by applying the ordinary
  projector $\gamma^\ord_{H'}$ and the local intertwiner $w^\ord_{\rm a}$,
  using the comparison of toric periods $Q$ and $Q^\ord$.  For
  representations of trivial weight satisfying (p-crys) and (ram) the
  formula holds by \cite[Theorem~B]{nonsplit}; Proposition~7.1.6 then
  propagates it to the general case, including exceptional $\Pi$, by
  density.

nodes.title: Universal Waldspurger formula
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Disegni, §7.2 ``A universal Waldspurger formula''.}
  This is the complementary picture over locally distinguished families
  attached to \emph{coherent} quaternion algebras over $F$.  Let $B$ be
  a totally definite quaternion algebra over $F$ with ramification set
  $\Sg$, let $G_{/\bQ}$ be the algebraic group with
  $G(R) = (B \otimes R)^\times$, and fix an embedding
  $\mathrm{e} \colon E \hookrightarrow B$; here all associated Shimura
  varieties are $0$-dimensional.  The normalised fundamental class of
  $Y$ yields an $H'(\bA)$-invariant functional $P_\Pi \colon \Pi \to L$
  and, over a locally distinguished Hida family $\cX$ for
  $(G \times H)'$ (isomorphic via Jacquet--Langlands to a family $\cX_0$
  for $(G_0 \times H)'$), an $H'(\bA^{p\infty})$-invariant
  $\scO_\cX$-linear functional
  $\cP \colon \Pi^{K^p,\ord}_{H'_\Sg} \to \scO_\cX$ interpolating the
  $P^\ord_{\Pi_z}$ at all $z \in \cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ satisfying~(wt).

  Abbreviate $\Pi^{(\iota)} := \Pi^{K^{p\prime},\ord,(\iota)}_{H'_\Sg}$,
  $\scO := \scO_\cX$, $\cK := \cK_\cX$.  There exists an open subset
  $\cX' \subset \cX$ containing $\cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ such that
  \[
    \frac{\cP(f_1) \cdot \cP^\iota(f_2)}{((f_3, f_4))}
    =
    \cL_p(\cV^\sharp)_{|\cX}
    \cdot
    \cQ\!\left(\frac{f_1 \otimes f_2}{f_3 \otimes f_4}\right),
  \]
  an equality of $\cK$-valued $\scO$-linear functionals on
  $(\Pi \otimes_\scO \Pi^\iota) \otimes_{\scO^\times}
  (\Pi \otimes_\scO \Pi^\iota)^{\times,-1}$.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:TheoremBord}
  \textit{Proof: see Disegni, §7.2.}
  As in the proof of \cref{thm:TheoremD}, the universal formula follows
  from its specialisations at all classical points $z \in
  \cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ satisfying~(wt).  Those classical Waldspurger
  formulas are known by modifying the main result of Waldspurger
  \cite{wald} exactly as in the ordinary-variant
  \cref{thm:TheoremBord}; since $\cX^{\mathrm{cl}}$ is Zariski dense in
  $\cX'$, the two $\scO$-linear functionals agreeing at every such point
  agree identically.
